mechanism_narrative: Higher INDPRO levels reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translate into positive earnings surprises and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO levels over a 2-year rolling window (t-statistic < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: An UNRATE level above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating household income expectations and rising corporate default risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE combined with falling INDPRO signals a recessionary supply-demand contraction that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE changes and INDPRO changes shows a t-statistic below 1.5 in predicting 60-day returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL accelerates expectations of further FEDFUNDS hikes, raising equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict negative 20-day returns with a t-statistic less than -1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Higher RSAFS levels indicate robust consumer spending and translate into positive earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS levels fail to show a statistically significant positive correlation with 5-day S&P 500 returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: HOUST below 1,200 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations when HOUST is below 1200.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL combined with falling FEDFUNDS produces a decline in real interest rates that compresses the equity discount rate and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS changes exhibits a negative sign or a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS above 140,000 thousand persons indicates a tight labor market that sustains consumer spending and wage-driven demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations when PAYEMS exceeds 140,000.
